title: Web Fuzzing → Content Discovery (feroxbuster / ffuf / gobuster)
body: The technique node for content discovery. The skill isn't running one tool - it's picking the RIGHT wordlist for the job and escalating deliberately: quick list -> raft -> directory-list -> tech-specific. Match extensions to the stack. And loop BACK to triage on any hit - enumeration is recursive.
cmd:
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# WEB FUZZING → run passes, then LOOP BACK to triage on any hit
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Found /admin? → go back to web_enum, fingerprint /admin, fuzz IT next.

# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# WORDLIST ARSENAL - the right list for the job beats a bigger list
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# CONFIRM paths on YOUR box first (filenames drift by SecLists version):
SL=/usr/share/seclists/Discovery/Web-Content
ls $SL | head -40 ; locate raft- 2>/dev/null | head

# -- THE LADDER (escalate in THIS order, don't just rerun raft) ------
# 1) QUICK first pass - tiny, instant, catches the obvious:
#    $SL/common.txt                         (~4700, dirb classic)
# 2) MAIN pass - real-world-derived, your default workhorse:
#    $SL/raft-medium-directories.txt        (dirs)
#    $SL/raft-medium-files.txt              (files WITH extensions)
# 3) ESCALATE if raft is dry - algorithmic, finds weird/legacy paths:
#    $SL/directory-list-2.3-medium.txt      (~220k, dirbuster)
#    $SL/directory-list-2.3-big.txt         (~1.2M, last resort)
#    $SL/raft-large-directories.txt         (bigger raft)
# 4) BIG general fallback:
#    $SL/big.txt

# -- HIGH-VALUE QUICK LISTS (run early - cheap, high hit rate) -------
# Known sensitive files/paths (.git/HEAD, backups, admin panels):
feroxbuster -u http://$ip -w $SL/quickhits.txt -d 1 -o ferox_quick.txt
# Paths harvested from real robots.txt files across the web:
feroxbuster -u http://$ip -w $SL/RobotsDisallowed/top10000.txt -d 1 -o ferox_robots.txt

# -- TECH-SPECIFIC (use AFTER you fingerprint the stack) ------------
# IIS / ASP.NET:
feroxbuster -u http://$ip -w $SL/IIS.fuzz.txt -x aspx,asp,config -o ferox_iis.txt
# Apache:
feroxbuster -u http://$ip -w $SL/apache.txt -o ferox_apache.txt
# PHP apps (common php filenames):
feroxbuster -u http://$ip -w $SL/Common-PHP-Filenames.txt -x php -o ferox_php.txt
# Tomcat (manager paths):
feroxbuster -u http://$ip -w $SL/tomcat.txt -o ferox_tomcat.txt
# CMS-specific (WordPress/Joomla/Drupal trees live here):
ls $SL/CMS/ ; feroxbuster -u http://$ip -w $SL/CMS/wp-plugins.fuzz.txt -o ferox_wp.txt
# API endpoints (if the app looks like a REST/JSON API):
feroxbuster -u http://$ip -w $SL/api/api-endpoints.txt -o ferox_api.txt

# -- EXTENSION STRATEGY (match -x to the stack) --------------------
# Don't fuzz php on IIS or aspx on Apache - waste of requests. Match it:
#   IIS/.NET   -> -x aspx,asp,ashx,asmx,config
#   Apache/PHP -> -x php,phtml,php3,php5,inc
#   Java       -> -x jsp,do,action
#   any/always -> -x txt,bak,old,zip,tar,gz,~,swp,backup   (loot!)
# Backup/config leaks specifically:
feroxbuster -u http://$ip -w $SL/raft-medium-files.txt -x bak,old,zip,tar.gz,config,~ -o ferox_backups.txt
feroxbuster -u http://$ip -w $SL/Common-DB-Backups.txt -o ferox_dbbak.txt

# -- PARAMETER fuzzing (hidden GET/POST params) --------------------
# When a page takes input but you don't know the param name:
ffuf -u 'http://$ip/page?FUZZ=test' -w $SL/burp-parameter-names.txt -fs BASELINE

# ── HOW FEROXBUSTER WORKS ────────────────────────────
# feroxbuster -u http://$ip  ← no trailing slash needed, no /FUZZ
# Recursive by default — finds /admin, auto-scans /admin/
# -d controls depth (default: 4)
# -n disables recursion for flat scan only
# -x appends extensions to every word — use with files wordlist
# --extract-links crawls response bodies for linked content too
# --filter-similar-to filters wildcard responses (box returns 200 for everything)

# ── WORDLIST DECISION TREE ────────────────────────────
# raft-medium-directories (30k)  → dirs pass, always start here
# raft-medium-files (real names) → files pass, different wordlist
# directory-list-2.3-medium (220k) → escalate if raft finds nothing
# dirbuster lists = dirs ONLY, no extensions — never use for files

# ── PASS 1: DIRECTORIES ───────────────────────────────
# raft-medium-directories = directory names without extensions
# feroxbuster recurses into found dirs automatically
feroxbuster -u http://$ip \
  -w /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/Web-Content/raft-medium-directories.txt \
  -d 3 --extract-links -o ferox_dirs_raft.txt

# HTTPS target (self-signed cert)
feroxbuster -u https://$ip -k \
  -w /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/Web-Content/raft-medium-directories.txt \
  -d 3 --extract-links -o ferox_dirs_raft.txt

# Wildcard filter — if box returns 200 for everything
# First get a wildcard URL to filter against:
feroxbuster -u http://$ip \
  -w /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/Web-Content/raft-medium-directories.txt \
  --filter-similar-to http://$ip/doesnotexist123 \
  -d 3 -o ferox_dirs_raft.txt

# ── PASS 2: FILES ─────────────────────────────────────
# raft-medium-files = actual filenames WITH extensions
# (config.php, index.bak, login.html etc)
feroxbuster -u http://$ip \
  -w /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/Web-Content/raft-medium-files.txt \
  -d 3 -o ferox_files.txt

# ── PASS 3: TARGETED EXTENSIONS ON KNOWN DIR ─────────
# once Pass 1 finds /admin, run extensions against it specifically
feroxbuster -u http://$ip/FOUND_DIR \
  -w /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/Web-Content/raft-medium-files.txt \
  -x php,html,txt,bak,old,zip -d 2 -o ferox_ext.txt

# ── PASS 4: ESCALATE — if raft found nothing ──────────
# directory-list-2.3-medium is 7x larger, pentest-derived
# finds CTF paths, legacy app dirs, obscure endpoints
feroxbuster -u http://$ip \
  -w /usr/share/wordlists/dirbuster/directory-list-2.3-medium.txt \
  -d 3 -o ferox_dirs_dirbuster.txt

# ── VHOST FUZZING ─────────────────────────────────────
# Virtual host enum — same IP, different Host header = different app
# Get baseline first (size of response with unknown host)
curl -s -H "Host: doesnotexist.example.com" http://$ip -o /dev/null -w "%{size_download}
"

# ffuf vhost fuzz — filter baseline size
ffuf -u http://$ip \
  -H "Host: FUZZ.$domain" \
  -w /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/DNS/subdomains-top1million-5000.txt \
  -fs BASELINE_SIZE -o ffuf_vhosts.txt

# If domain unknown — try common patterns
ffuf -u http://$ip \
  -H "Host: FUZZ" \
  -w /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/DNS/subdomains-top1million-5000.txt \
  -fs BASELINE_SIZE

# gobuster vhost mode
gobuster vhost -u http://$ip \
  -w /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/DNS/subdomains-top1million-5000.txt \
  --append-domain -t 40

# Add found vhosts to /etc/hosts then enumerate each
echo "$ip found.domain.com" | sudo tee -a /etc/hosts
feroxbuster -u http://found.domain.com \
  -w /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/Web-Content/raft-medium-directories.txt \
  -d 3 -o ferox_vhost.txt

# ── GOBUSTER — fast single-pass fallback ──────────────
gobuster dir -u http://$ip \
  -w /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/Web-Content/raft-medium-directories.txt \
  -t 40 -o gobuster_dirs.txt
gobuster dir -u http://$ip \
  -w /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/Web-Content/raft-medium-files.txt \
  -t 40 -o gobuster_files.txt

# ── FFUF — fine-grained control ───────────────────────
curl http://$ip/doesnotexist123 -s -o /dev/null -w "%{size_download}
"
ffuf -u http://$ip/FUZZ \
  -w /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/Web-Content/raft-medium-directories.txt \
  -mc 200,301,302,403 -fs BASELINE_SIZE -o ffuf_dirs.txt
ffuf -u http://$ip/FUZZ \
  -w /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/Web-Content/raft-medium-files.txt \
  -mc 200,301,302 -fs BASELINE_SIZE -o ffuf_files.txt
# files in a known dir:
ffuf -u http://$ip/FOUND_DIR/FUZZ \
  -w /usr/share/seclists/Discovery/Web-Content/raft-medium-files.txt \
  -mc 200,301,302 -fs BASELINE_SIZE

# ── TECH FINGERPRINT ──────────────────────────────────
whatweb http://$ip
curl -sv http://$ip 2>&1 | grep -i "server\|x-powered\|set-cookie\|location"
curl http://$ip/robots.txt
curl http://$ip/sitemap.xml
# nikto is slow and noisy — background it or run last
nikto -h http://$ip -o nikto.txt &
warn: Escalate wordlists deliberately - common -> raft-medium -> directory-list-2.3-medium - don't just rerun the same list bigger. Once you know the stack, switch to tech-specific lists (IIS.fuzz/apache/Common-PHP-Filenames/tomcat/CMS) and match -x extensions to it (aspx on IIS, php on Apache). Run quickhits and RobotsDisallowed early - cheap and high-hit. Confirm SecLists paths on your box (ls $SL) - filenames vary by version. Check verbs (OPTIONS) BEFORE fuzzing - PUT/MOVE/MKCOL in the Allow header means WebDAV, often a direct upload->RCE that fuzzing never finds. feroxbuster recurses automatically — no trailing slash or /FUZZ needed. If every path returns 200, use --filter-similar-to with a known-bad URL to filter wildcards. If raft finds nothing escalate to directory-list-2.3-medium — OSCP boxes frequently hide paths there. Vhost fuzzing is often the hidden path — always check when you have a domain name or SSL cert CN. If responses come back all-000, inconsistent, or every path errors, the server is choking or rate-limiting — DON'T conclude 'nothing there'. Drop threads (-t 10 or lower), add --rate-limit, or retry single-threaded with curl; a fragile single-threaded service or a WAF will fail a fast scan and pass a slow one.
